title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Justin Slaten gave up four hits, four runs, including two home runs, as Red Sox fell to Twins 8-6.
Manager Chad Tracy pulled starter Payton Tolle after six innings and 85 pitches despite his solid performance.
Boston's offense scored six runs but was overshadowed by Minnesota's late surge, losing the series opener.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens threw the first pitch at Fenway Park before Red Sox vs. Twins, with his son Kody catching.
Red Sox legend Clemens, age 63, participated in a bobblehead giveaway commemorating his 20-strikeout games.
Kody Clemens, playing for the Twins, hit a home run at Fenway in his first career game there last May.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White was named to the NBA All-Defensive First Team for the 2025-26 season, his first career nod.
White recorded a career-high 98 blocks and 88 steals, leading all guards with 552 contested shots.
Celtics finished second in the Eastern Conference with a 56-26 record, largely without Jayson Tatum.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Tatiana Maslany stars in 'Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed,' streaming on Apple TV.
'Glory' and 'Arco' highlight new movies streaming on Netflix and Hulu, respectively.
'The Mandalorian' and 'Strangers with Candy' are notable TV shows available on Disney+ and Paramount+.

title: Chad Tracy provides injury updates for Garrett Crochet, Roman Anthony
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox's Garrett Crochet is set for a throwing session this weekend and live batting practice next week.
Roman Anthony is experiencing pain tolerance issues after swinging, with no clear return date from the injured list.
Trevor Story underwent sports hernia surgery, sidelining him for approximately 6-10 weeks.

title: Sam Kennedy says Red Sox looking to add bat ‘earlier than ever’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox are discussing early trade talks to address offensive struggles, per President Sam Kennedy.
Key departures and injuries have led to Boston's low runs and home runs stats this season.
The team is two games from a wild-card spot, contemplating whether to be buyers or sellers.

title: Mock draft predicts Red Sox will select Bishop Feehan star in first round
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: MLB.com's Jonathan Mayo predicts Red Sox will pick Brody Bumila at No. 20 in the draft.
Bumila, a Bishop Feehan lefty, is known for his 6-foot-9 frame and powerful fastball.
Keith Law and Kiley McDaniel suggest Bumila might be a second-round draft talent.

title: Red Sox’ Trevor Story undergoes sports hernia surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox shortstop Trevor Story underwent successful hernia surgery and will likely miss 6-10 weeks.
Story's surgery marks his third procedure since joining the Red Sox, following shoulder and elbow surgeries.
In Story's absence, Isaiah Kiner-Falefa, Andruw Monasterio, and Nick Sogard will cover shortstop duties.

title: Peter Wolf left one Boston art school to form the J. Geils Band. Six decades later, he earned an honorary doctorate from another.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Peter Wolf, a former Boston Museum School of Fine Arts student, received an honorary doctorate from Massachusetts College of Art and Design.
Wolf, once known for college-hopping as a pretend art student, eventually left school to form the legendary J. Geils Band.
Peter Wolf's future projects include releasing a new album and potentially writing another book following his memoir 'Waiting on the Moon.'

title: Boston 25 hires Lauren Walsh for newly-created role
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Lauren Walsh is joining Boston 25 as a sports media journalist, a new role at the station.
Walsh is a Syracuse graduate and former NESN intern with experience in Nashville and Winston-Salem.
She expressed excitement to cover the Boston teams she grew up supporting, but her start date is not yet set.
